# traceforce_datalakes (Data Source)

Retrieves information about existing Traceforce datalakes.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Get all datalakes
data "traceforce_datalakes" "all" {}

# Filter datalakes by project
data "traceforce_datalakes" "project_datalakes" {
  filter = {
    project_id = traceforce_project.production.id
  }
}

# Use datalake data in other resources
resource "traceforce_source_app" "new_app" {
  name        = "new-salesforce"
  datalake_id = data.traceforce_datalakes.all.datalakes[0].id
  type        = "Salesforce"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Optional

- `filter` (Object) Filter criteria for datalakes (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--filter))

### Read-Only

- `datalakes` (List of Object) List of datalakes (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--datalakes))

<a id="nestedatt--filter"></a>
### Nested Schema for `filter`

Optional:

- `project_id` (String) Filter by project ID
- `status` (String) Filter by datalake status
- `type` (String) Filter by datalake type

<a id="nestedatt--datalakes"></a>
### Nested Schema for `datalakes`

Read-Only:

- `id` (String) System generated unique identifier
- `name` (String) Datalake name
- `project_id` (String) ID of the project containing this datalake
- `type` (String) Datalake type
- `status` (String) Current datalake status
- `created_at` (String) Creation timestamp
- `updated_at` (String) Last update timestamp
